records.ety: [F., fr. L. risibilis, fr. ridere, risum, to laugh. Cf. Ridiculous.]
usage: Risible differs from ludicrous as species from genus; ludicrous expressing that which is playful and sportive; risible, that which may excite laughter. Risible differs from ridiculous, as the latter implies something contemptuous, and risible does not.

defs: Having the faculty or power of laughing; disposed to laugh.
quotes: Laughing is our busines, . . . it has been made the definition of man that he is risible.

defs: Exciting laughter; worthy to be laughed at; amusing.
quotes: I hope you find nothing risible in my complaisance.

defs: Used in, or expressing, laughter; as, risible muscles.
notes: ☞ Risible is sometimes used as a noun, in the plural, for the feeling of amusement and for the muscles and other organs used in laughing, collectively; as, unable to control one's risibles.
